name: Siemens PLC Libraries
desc: A port of Honeywell DCS control blocks to Siemens PLC — bringing familiar DCS semantics (PID, DEVCTL, AI/AO/DI/DO) into TIA Portal as reusable FC/FB units, usable standalone or as part of a historian stack.
problem: Plants migrating from Honeywell DCS (Experion, TDC) to Siemens PLCs lose their control block library and have to rebuild logic from scratch. This library ports the core Honeywell control modules — PID (closed-loop control), DEVCTL (discrete device management for pumps, motors, and valves), and AI/AO/DI/DO (analog and digital I/O with engineering unit scaling, alarming, and signal conditioning) — to Siemens S7-300/1500 as native FC/FB blocks in TIA Portal. Engineers familiar with Honeywell configuration get equivalent behaviour without relearning control semantics.
audience: Automation engineers and system integrators migrating from Honeywell DCS to Siemens PLC, or building new systems that require DCS-grade control block semantics on a PLC platform.

name: Imbra Connect
desc: Lightweight protocol SDKs for rapid prototyping and OT connectivity — production-ready at typical process sampling rates, with a clean interface for crafting, parsing, and inspecting protocol frames.
problem: Connecting to industrial devices should not require days of setup or deep knowledge of protocol internals. Each SDK installs with a single command and exposes a clean packet-level interface — letting engineers read live data, craft custom frames, and build connectivity agents without low-level bit manipulation. Designed for historian agents, integration scripts, and proof-of-concept work that needs to become production code quickly.
audience: Embedded engineers, system integrators, MES developers building device connectivity layers.

name: ImBrain™
desc: An open-architecture historian for small and medium-sized plants — minimal well-defined core, open below via agents, open above via plugins. Free to try, paid plugins and optional support.
problem: Most industrial historians are expensive, closed, and sized for enterprise. ImBrain is built on an open hourglass architecture — like TCP/IP, it defines a minimal, well-specified core with open contracts on both sides: plant-facing agents connect any data source below, and application plugins consume the data above. The core and agents are open source. Engineers are encouraged to install, explore, and run it at no cost. Value is delivered through plugins that work with the data, plus optional installation and maintenance services for teams that need them.
audience: Engineers and operations teams at small and medium-sized industrial plants looking for a capable historian without enterprise licensing costs.

name: CodeWithBranko
desc: A dedicated platform for engineers working at the OT/IT boundary — practical courses, tutorials, and guides on industrial protocols, PLC programming, historian architecture, and applied AI/ML.
problem: Most industrial training is either too academic to be useful in the field or too vendor-specific to transfer across platforms. CodeWithBranko is built around real engineering problems encountered across automation, connectivity, and software projects — giving engineers practical skills they can apply immediately, regardless of which platform or protocol they are working with.
audience: Automation engineers, embedded developers, and software engineers stepping into OT environments — or IT engineers navigating their first industrial project.
